edge_cases_considered: MCP manager direct handle paths bypass `SharedMcpPool::call_tool`, so acquisition must be in `McpManager::call_tool` too, not only in the pool.
Connect-on-first-call may enter after cached tool advertisement; acquiring at `McpManager::call_tool` entry covers both connection latency and the subsequent call.
Detached background tasks intentionally outlive daemon and must not hold process leases, otherwise idle exit could be pinned forever by work that should survive independently.
`ActivityLeaseGuard::Drop` cannot be async, so the trait release path must be synchronous and app-core must use a sync mutex/atomic table, not tokio locks.
Backcompat/test constructors need a noop authority so existing MCP/background tests do not all need immediate server scaffolding.
Shutdown refusal changes background spawn APIs that currently return `BackgroundTaskInfo` directly, so migration should add `try_` APIs before removing or changing old signatures.
evidence: docs/fork/ideal-base/reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md:55-66 identifies the current proposed app-core authority placement and lower `jcode-base` MCP paths, plus the dependency-cycle issue.

docs/fork/ideal-base/reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md:84-89 requires an implementable inversion seam, either lower neutral process-activity guard or injected callback/trait into `McpManager` and `BackgroundTaskManager`.
docs/fork/ideal-base/reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md:278-280 marks non-detached background and MCP calls as unresolved coverage gaps.
docs/fork/ideal-base/ACCEPTANCE_STANDARD.md:17-23 requires idle exit to require zero clients and zero active work leases covering background jobs and MCP calls.

crates/jcode-app-core/Cargo.toml:85-89 and crates/jcode-base/Cargo.toml:103-104 show both app-core and base can depend on `jcode-core`; crates/jcode-base/Cargo.toml:7-12 and crates/jcode-app-core/src/lib.rs:12-24 show base is foundational and re-exported upward.
crates/jcode-base/src/mcp/tool.rs:49-58 is the registered tool execution path into `McpManager::call_tool`.
crates/jcode-base/src/mcp/manager.rs:48-54 stores both pooled handles and per-session owned clients; manager.rs:348-388 contains the pooled, owned, and connect-on-first-call tool-call paths.
crates/jcode-base/src/mcp/pool.rs:231-243 exposes direct shared-pool `call_tool`; pool.rs:391-408 exposes the pool singleton helpers.

crates/jcode-base/src/mcp/client.rs:36-60 provides the existing RAII permit pattern; client.rs:166-174 shows `McpClient` owns the child and optional permit; client.rs:373-383 and :409-412 show shutdown/drop kills the child.

crates/jcode-base/src/background.rs:35-39 defines `BackgroundTaskManager` in lower base; background.rs:439-459 and :642-657 write non-detached Running statuses; background.rs:584-600 and :787-803 insert live running tasks.
crates/jcode-base/src/background.rs:248-281 and crates/jcode-app-core/src/server.rs:1171-1185 prove non-detached orphan reconciliation exists because live futures die on crash/reload.
crates/jcode-base/src/background.rs:357-380 and docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F01/design.md:62-65 prove detached tasks are deliberately not lease holders.
crates/jcode-app-core/src/server/lifecycle.rs:90-96 and :159-178 prove idle exit currently consults only client count and exits directly.

crates/jcode-app-core/src/server/util.rs:39-43 and crates/jcode-app-core/src/tool/mod.rs:758-778 are concrete app-core composition points for injecting the activity authority into the shared MCP pool and managers.
open_questions: Whether maintainers prefer reusing `jcode-core/src/activity.rs` or adding a new even narrower `jcode-activity-core` crate. The analysis recommends `jcode-core` because both target crates already depend on it, but a new crate would also satisfy the dependency direction at the cost of workspace churn.

Whether `BackgroundTaskManager::global()` should remain process-global with a set-once activity authority, or whether app-core should thread an explicit manager handle through all spawn/adopt call sites. The explicit manager is cleaner, but likely larger migration.

Whether lease refusal during shutdown should fail background tool calls immediately or create a terminal failed background-task status row for UI continuity. The lower API supports either, but app-core product semantics should choose one.
artifact.validation: Read-only validation only. Commands used: `rg`/`agentgrep` to locate MCP/background/design paths; `nl -ba | sed` to capture exact line-numbered excerpts for review/design docs, Cargo manifests, MCP manager/pool/tool/client files, background manager/model files, server lifecycle/runtime/reload/composition files. No edits, builds, or tests were run because this node requested analysis only.
what_i_did_not_check: I did not enumerate every background spawn/adopt caller in app-core for exact migration count.
I did not inspect all `Registry::register_mcp_tools_for_dir` call sites beyond representative server/headless/runtime composition points.
I did not implement or compile the proposed API because the task explicitly required analysis only and no edits.
